title: Dryoathyrium erectum
intro: Dryoathyrium erectum, also known as the Japanese climbing fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryoathyrium erectum include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dryoathyrium erectum is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Dryoathyrium erectum is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryoathyrium, which is a group of ferns found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, commonly known as wood ferns or lady ferns. Dryoathyrium erectum is a unique species within this family, characterized by its upright growth habit and distinctive leaf shape. The classification of Dryoathyrium erectum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns and its place within the broader plant kingdom.
